title: docmend manifest record (DR-004)
description: One line of the append-only JSON Lines manifest written during apply or restore (FR-006, adr-0019 manifest 2.0). This schema validates a SINGLE mutation record - line 1 of the file is a docmend/manifest-header document validated by manifest-header.schema.json. Each mutation appends (and fsyncs) an 'intent' record BEFORE any corpus name is touched and a terminal record after (journal-every-mutation); records carry the durable object identities post-kill adjudication verifies against. 2.0 is a clean break: no 1.x read path exists (adr-0019).

docmend_id.description: Stable document identity (UUIDv7, adr-0008); `docmend restore` replays records per docmend.id in LIFO order (IR-008).

seq.description: Monotonic record number within this run's manifest, starting at 1 — establishes the LIFO replay order and makes truncation detectable.

operation.description: What mutated: 'rename' moved the file without content change; 'rewrite' replaced content in place; 'rename_and_rewrite' did both as one atomic action.

original_path.description: Absolute path — restore locates files from the manifest alone (IR-008).

target_path.description: Absolute path — restore locates files from the manifest alone (IR-008).

backup_path.description: Verified backup copy location when tool-written backups were enabled (FR-006); null under the Git/external/low-risk-opt-in preservation strategies. Absolute path — restore locates files from the manifest alone (IR-008).

after_sha256.description: Hash of the file as written; equals before_sha256 for a pure rename. Null when result is 'failed' (the original is intact, spec 10.4). For result 'intent' (1.3) this is the EXPECTED output hash, recorded before the mutation.

result.description: 2.0 (adr-0019): EVERY mutation kind journals 'intent' before any corpus name is touched and a terminal ('applied' | 'failed') after. A dangling intent (no terminal after it in the chain) is adjudicated from disk state against the persisted identities. A terminal with no same-set intent is provisionally legal at set scope and must close exactly one earlier set's dangling intent at chain scope.

overwritten_sha256.description: 1.1 (OQ-035): hash of the pre-existing target this mutation clobbered under the overwrite collision policy; null otherwise.

overwritten_backup_path.description: 1.1 (OQ-035): verified backup of the clobbered target when tool backups were enabled; null otherwise.

undoes_action_id.description: 2.0 restore lineage: the exact apply action this inverse undoes; null in apply-kind sets. Set together with undoes_run_id or not at all.

undoes_run_id.description: 2.0 restore lineage: the run that applied the action this inverse undoes.

source_identity.description: 2.0 (adr-0019/adr-0020): exact (st_dev, st_ino) of the validated source object, persisted in the intent before mutation; null on pre-mutation failed records.

target_identity.description: 2.0: identity of the overwrite target when one existed at commit; null otherwise.

expected_published_identity.description: 2.0: identity the published output will have - the staged inode for replacement publishes, the source inode for pure renames. What post-publish adjudication lstat-compares against.
